title: --sudo option: 6-layer elevation architecture with pulse tracing (v5.14)
desc: 9-layer --sudo elevation architecture (v5.14.7). Post-UAC 100ms handshake reuses pre-booting admin Eye (Layer 8, prevents week-long broadcast-close churn). User Eye pipe refuses --sudo with FALLBACK exit 125 (Layer 7). IsAvailable → Ping migration everywhere (Layer 9). Windows cmd restricted-process hang (StSess32 30s) + fast-fail budget issues RESOLVED. Pulse ring-buffer + reason dump make UAC prompts traceable.
steps: LAYER 1 — MCP Launcher pre-execution gate (McpProxy.cs stdin router). Pre-detects --sudo in JSON-RPC requests; preemptively calls SpawnAdminCore if admin not running. Existing reactive UIPI path untouched.

LAYER 3 — eye --sudo non-destructive force-launch. Replaced old destructive kill+runas path. Now calls LaunchElevatedEye side-by-side. Old user Eye preserved; new admin Eye cleans up abnormal Eye itself.
LAYER 4 — Eye auto-launch --sudo inheritance (AppBotEyeCommands.cs). Guardian args append --sudo when Program.IsElevated()==true. Admin Core respawning Eye → new Eye also admin+--sudo marker.

LAYER 5 — Pre-spawn hot-swap piggyback (v5.14). LaunchElevatedEye, before runas+UAC, checks for wkappbot-core.new.exe. If pending: rename current exe → backup, rename .new.exe → .exe. Admin Eye starts from latest code. Log: [ELEVATION:HOT-SWAP] pending .new.exe detected — promoting before admin spawn

LAYER 6 — Launcher-side --sudo bypass + stderr passthrough (v5.14.x hotfix). isSudoRequest in Eye pipe delegation skip prevents stale user Eye from handling. showStderr implicitly true when --sudo in args so PULSE tracing visible.

LAYER 7 — User Eye refuses --sudo via pipe with FALLBACK marker (v5.14.7, commit 9220f66). EyeCmdPipeServer.HandleClientAsync checks args.Contains(--sudo) BEFORE dispatch. Responds with [EYE:FALLBACK] lines + exit 125 sentinel. Reason: user Eye is not elevated and proxying via admin Eye is caller responsibility. Belt-and-suspenders: Launcher already skips pipe delegation for --sudo but belt-and-suspenders guard prevents silent wrong-scope dispatch.

LAYER 8 — LaunchElevatedEye post-UAC 100ms handshake (v5.14.7, commit a4f4f69). AFTER the runas StartTracked call (UAC approved OR cancelled), UNCONDITIONAL ElevatedEyeClient.Ping(100) check. If responsive → reuse and return true (skip our spawn). If unresponsive AND UAC cancelled → clean fail. If unresponsive AND UAC approved → proceed with spawn poll. Rationale: Eye boot time and UAC dialog time frequently overlap — a pre-existing booting Eye may be ready by the time user answers UAC. Without this handshake we spawn a duplicate, which fires broadcast-close on the just-finished one and kills it mid-request. This is the exact churn that caused weeks of --sudo regressions.

LAYER 9 — IsAvailable → Ping migration (v5.14.7, commit 4664def). ElevatedEyeClient.IsAvailable() only checks pipe file existence; the file can outlive a crashed server. Ping(100) performs an actual ConnectAsync and is the authoritative liveness check. Sites migrated: Program.cs (eye --sudo force-launch), ElevatedEyeProxy.cs lines 405/452/484 (all skip-launch decisions), poll loop in LaunchElevatedEye. IsAvailable kept only for post-spawn poll semantics in some paths.

PULSE TRACING — step-by-step debug via stderr. Program.cs uses PulseStep.Init/Line/Finish throughout --sudo block. Force mode (Line) always emits. Ring buffer (20 lines) captured in PulseStep.GetRecentTrail — used by UAC prompt.

UAC REASON + TRAIL DUMP (ElevatedEyeProxy.LaunchElevatedEye). Before UAC, prints reason + last 5 pulse lines. Sites provide contextual reasons so user understands WHY admin is needed before they approve.

DEBUGGING CHECKLIST. When --sudo misbehaves: (1) wkappbot --sudo <cmd> 2>&1 and inspect PULSE output, (2) check trail before UAC to identify which step requested admin, (3) tasklist for wkappbot-core cores (expect user+admin), (4) Test-Path admin pipe wkappbot_elevated, (5) run tests/sudo/test-mcp-sudo-gate.sh.

RESOLVED: windows cmd 30s hang on restricted process (v5.14.7, commit bd26a28). GetProcessPerf wraps Process.GetProcessById in a 300ms worker-thread timeout. Restricted processes like Shinhan StSess32 (NzSess32MainWndCN class) previously stalled the entire enumeration for 30 seconds. Now caps at 300ms with zero metrics on timeout. Closed Bug 1 from v5.14.x known issues.

RESOLVED: --sudo fast-fail 1.5s too tight (v5.14.7, commit 13dbf3b). Bumped to 3s. New binary subprocess latency is ~1.3s (windows cmd + pipe overhead); 1.5s budget tripped on healthy admin Eye, triggering recovery LaunchElevatedEye + broadcast-close cycle that killed the working admin Eye mid-request.
author: claude
